notes_md:
**quay** = to turn or rotate something, or to turn your body/head. It’s about changing direction or position.

As a verb, it often goes with physical objects or parts of the body (đầu, người, xe, chìa khóa). It can also mean “to film/shoot” (quay phim, quay video) — very common in modern life.


It also combines with particles like **quay lại** (go back/return) and **quay về** (return to a place).  
Close in meaning to **xoay** (rotate/spin), but *xoay* feels more like spinning around an axis, while *quay* is broader and more everyday.

translation.en: Do you want to turn back?

content.sentence: Chúng ta cần ___ lại để xem.

translation.en: We need to turn back to see.

notes:
quay = to turn or rotate something, or to turn your body/head. It’s about changing direction or position.

As a verb, it often goes with physical objects or parts of the body (đầu, người, xe, chìa khóa). It can also mean “to film/shoot” (quay phim, quay video) — very common in modern life.

It also combines with particles like quay lại (go back/return) and quay về (return to a place).  
Close in meaning to xoay (rotate/spin), but xoay feels more like spinning around an axis, while quay is broader and more everyday.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Can you turn this chair slightly to the right?
When she heard her name called, she turned to look at me immediately.
I forgot to turn the door lock before going out this morning.

example.en: We will come back here tomorrow.
example.vi: Chúng ta sẽ quay lại đây vào ngày mai.

definitions.en: To turn around or return to a previous place.
definitions.vi: Quay lại một nơi nào đó.
definitions.es: Girar o regresar a un lugar anterior.

exampleSentences: Anh có thể quay nhẹ cái ghế này sang bên phải được không?
Khi chị nghe thấy tiếng gọi, chị quay lại nhìn em ngay lập tức.
Em quên quay khóa cửa trước khi ra ngoài sáng nay.
